title: Defining the future of thermal energy networks
description: Latest updates and insights defining the future of thermal energy networks and the geothermal industry.
content: The Denver Museum of Nature & Science was drilling for geothermal energy, aiming to switch from natural gas, and guess what they found 763 feet down in their parking lot? A 67.5-million-year-old dinosaur bone! This incredibly rare discovery, made during a geothermal test drill, shows the amazing hidden depths we can tap into when exploring clean energy solutions. Who knew the path to resilient, stable infrastructure could also lead to prehistoric marvels?! Link: https://aol.it/4lITxqT

Exciting insights from Plumbing & Mechanical on the future of building energy! Our very own Greg Cunniff P.E. shares how Radiant Heating And Cooling systems are central to the next generation of infrastructure. https://bit.ly/3Uh1R52

The Climate Control Group, Inc. (CCG) announced this week the appointment of Daniel Ellis as Chief Executive Officer. We are eager to see how Dan Ellis elevates The Climate Control Group. If you’d like to learn more, click the link! https://bit.ly/3Zdmz94

The geothermal industry is at the cusp of significant transformation, buoyed by strong support from President Donald Trump’s administration. Unlike other energy verticals facing policy hurdles, geothermal is being highlighted as a strategic player in achieving energy resilience due to its reliability, efficiency, and compatibility with existing energy systems. The link to the article: https://bit.ly/4l7cIu3

Check out Jay Egg here in Miller Proctor Nickolas, Inc.‘s latest installment in our docuseries for the Empire Technology Prize, hosted by The Clean Fight. Take a look at this episode focused on the Oilon Heat Pumps, which are incredibly well presented in the Mobile Demonstration Trailer as it traveled to New York Geothermal Energy Organization in Saratoga Springs. https://bit.ly/43i7x4g

“Let’s take a look at industry pioneer Jack DiEnna and reminisce on his impact on geothermal. He’s inspired many of the leaders we have today and made waves since his start in the world of geothermal. Take a look at this article to learn more about his history and influence!” https://bit.ly/4kwSdGO

Thermal Energy Networks are utility-scale infrastructure projects that connect multiple buildings into a shared network with sources of thermal energy like geothermal boreholes, surface water, and wastewater. In this video, you will learn all of the essential, foundational information to get you comfortable with the topic of TENs.” https://bit.ly/4hm1EXC

What’s a Thermal Energy Network? See a New York City Case Study There are many considerations when designing a geothermal network. Take 15 minutes to see a few of them in this short presentation. https://bit.ly/4hm1EXC

Utilizing AI Data Centers to Heat Surf and Wave Parks By heating the water using thermal exchange technology from data centers, parks can maintain ideal water temperatures regardless of the season. This creates a more inviting atmosphere that encourages longer daily use and year-round attendance. https://bit.ly/4izp4t9
High-rise multi-family apartments to pioneer first-ever geothermal/wastewater heat recovery system

There were five thermal energy networks conferences in 2025, which only goes to show that the boom in TENs is picking up and shows no signs of slowing down anytime soon! Read about it here https://bit.ly/3DSApWZ

Energy Secretary Chris Wright throws support behind geothermal boom “In their pitch to the right, geothermal leaders have emphasized the extensive overlap between oil and gas technology and geothermal, as well as work it offers for fossil fuels drilling crews idled in an increasingly efficient oil sector, where fewer than ever rigs pour out record amounts of oil. In his keynote, Wright himself hit that point. The technology that squeezed oil and gas from shale, opening an era of record American oil and gas production, was “tailor made for geothermal.” https://bit.ly/41spIlp -MAGMA (Making America Geothermal: Modern Advances).
Underground Thermal Energy Networks May Be About to Have Their Moment These climate-friendly heating-and-cooling systems are drawing support from states, cities, utilities and developers
Municipal and industry leaders discuss how this mid-size city is taking progressive action on resource-efficient energy

Apopka, FL is setting an example by including geothermal systems in a new apartment complex and opening up the opportunity for people who likely don’t know much, if anything about ground source heating and cooling to see the advantages for themselves.
Underground Thermal Energy Networks May Be About to Have Their Moment These climate-friendly heating-and-cooling systems are drawing support from states, cities, utilities and developers
Thermal Energy Network (TEN) Symposium in Rochester was a Big Success! Read about it here https://bit.ly/4cH6M7w

The Five on Fire awards that recognizes geothermal champions, risk takers, and pioneers who are challenging the status quo, engaging new minds, inspiring others to act, and causing chain reactions that are taking geothermal exponential.

How do Municipalities decide it’s time to optimize and electrify their built environment? Watch this video to see the reasoning and questions that are asked, and the answers shared. You will understand how Geothermal Heat Pumps are used to create Thermal Energy Networks in your city, village or community – https://lnkd.in/ggsdGHVn

title: Mission Statement
description: “The mission of Egg Geo is to facilitate national and worldwide adoption of geothermal exchange technologies through advocacy, engineering expertise, and geothermal exchange curriculum.” New York’s Thermal Energy Networks Law This TEN legislation took about seven years from concept to passage.
content: “The mission of Egg Geo is to facilitate national and worldwide adoption of geothermal exchange technologies through advocacy, engineering expertise, and geothermal exchange curriculum.”

This TEN legislation took about seven years from concept to passage.

title: About Egg Geo
description: Egg Geo is a global leader in geothermal exchange consulting for geothermal energy districts, datacenters, institutions, and multifamily complexes for clients ranging from developers and ESG funds to governments and other entities. Our mission is to ensure our clients optimize their capabilities to generate effective heating and cooling in buildings for and transitioning to stable
content: Egg Geo is a global leader in geothermal exchange consulting for geothermal energy districts, datacenters, institutions, and multifamily complexes for clients ranging from developers and ESG funds to governments and other entities. Our mission is to ensure our clients optimize their capabilities to generate effective heating and cooling in buildings for and transitioning to stable and resilient energy.

Clients benefit from our ability to plan and technically oversee complex energy delivery projects through our multidisciplinary approach. Our approach builds on the solid foundation of more than three decades of geothermal industry experience, providing effective and robust solutions for our clients’ objectives.
